name: che-word-mcp
description: v3.15.2 closes Bundle A polish from #61 R2 verify (Refs #69 #73 #74 #75): #69 (bug) insert_paragraph append message reports body.children index instead of getParagraphs().count - 1 (mis-reported in docs with tables/SDTs by skipping table children); #74 (bug) insert_image_from_path debug log labels after_image_id correctly (was silently labeled 'index'); #73 (test) regression pin for equation F5 partial-dict guard (existed since v3.15.1 but was untested); #75 (docs) clarifies '3 insert tools' wording — scope is the 3 #61-target tools (insert_paragraph / insert_equation / insert_image_from_path); insert_caption is a 4th insert tool with its own anchor set including after_table_index, intentionally outside this unification scope. Tests: 194 → 196 (0 fail / 9 skip). No behavior change in normal call paths. No ooxml-swift dep bump (still v0.20.5). Swift-native MCP server for Microsoft Word document (.docx) manipulation. v3.15.1 closes verify findings F1+F2+F3+F5 from v3.15.0 6-AI ensemble (5 Claude reviewers + Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh): F1 (P1) after_image_id anchor missing on insert_paragraph + insert_equation + insert_image_from_path — lib InsertLocation.afterImageId existed since #44 but only insert_caption was wired; F2 (P1) into_table_cell missing on insert_equation — display mode equation is structurally a new paragraph; F3 (P2) equation success message lacked anchor info — same v3.14.4 LOOKUP-over-claim pattern (now mirrors paragraph: 'Inserted equation (display mode: true, after text X (instance N))'); F5 (P2) malformed into_table_cell partial dict (e.g. {table_index: 0} missing row+col) silently fell through to next anchor / append — now returns structured 'Error: into_table_cell requires all three fields' in all 3 tools. Anchor priority unified across all 3 #61-target tools (insert_paragraph / insert_equation / insert_image_from_path): into_table_cell > after_image_id > after_text > before_text > index > append. Inline equation rejects all anchor params with structured error (anchor semantics ambiguous; use paragraph_index). Tests: Issue61V315PointReleaseTests (9 sub-tests covering F1×3 tools / F2 display+inline / F3 message format / F5 in 2 tools). Suite 185 → 194 (0 fail / 9 pre-existing skips). No ooxml-swift dep bump (still v0.20.5). Follow-up issues for F4 (inline equation more general design) / F6 (text anchor traversal into tables/SDTs) / F7 (getParagraphs index mismatch — pre-existing) / F8 (error tool-prefix) / F9 (conflicting anchor warnings) / F10 (text_instance<=0 normalization). v3.15.0 closes #61 — insert_paragraph and insert_equation now accept the same anchor parameters as insert_image_from_path: after_text / before_text / text_instance / into_table_cell (insert_paragraph also). Pre-fix MCP layer silently dropped these arguments — JSON schema accepted them but handler dispatch ignored them, so calls fell through to legacy paragraph_index path or appended at end. Lib API Document.insertParagraph(_: at: InsertLocation) has supported all six anchor cases since #44; this release closes the MCP-side wire-up gap. Anchor priority mirrors insert_image_from_path: into_table_cell > after_text > before_text > index > append. Errors (textNotFound / tableIndexOutOfRange / tableCellOutOfRange) return structured messages instead of silent fallthrough. insert_equation in inline mode (display_mode=false) explicitly rejects anchors with structured error — semantics ambiguous between 'append OMML run into existing para' vs 'insert new para before/after target'; use paragraph_index for inline placement. Tests: Issue61InsertParagraphAnchorsSmokeTests (5 sub-tests covering after_text / before_text / text_instance disambiguation / into_table_cell / textNotFound error) + Issue61InsertEquationAnchorsSmokeTests (4 sub-tests covering after_text + before_text in display mode / inline mode rejection / textNotFound error). Suite 176 → 185 (0 fail). No ooxml-swift dep bump (v0.20.5 has all needed lib APIs from #44). Backward compatible — all anchor parameters optional; existing index / paragraph_index callers unchanged. v3.14.5 closes Refs #63 verify F1 P1: extends findBodyChildContainingText (used by InsertLocation.afterText / .beforeText resolution) to mirror Document.replaceInParagraphSurfaces surface coverage. Pre-fix v3.14.4 only fixed REPLACE path (replace_text); LOOKUP path (insert_image_from_path / insert_paragraph / insert_caption before_text/after_text) still threw textNotFound against SDT-wrapped anchors despite v3.14.4 CHANGELOG claim. ooxml-swift v0.20.5 adds TextReplacementEngine.flatTextOfContentXML (read-only XML walker mirroring replaceInContentXML flattening rules) + Paragraph.flattenedDisplayText extension covering runs + hyperlinks + fieldSimples + alternateContents + contentControls (recursive into nested SDT). findBodyChildContainingText now uses flattenedDisplayText. Suite 693 → 696 ooxml-swift / 174 → 176 che-word-mcp. Bumps ooxml-swift v0.20.4 → v0.20.5. Backward compatible — strict superset of pre-fix lookup behavior. v3.14.4 fixes replace_text on inline <w:sdt> content controls (closes #63): Document.replaceInParagraphSurfaces previously covered paragraph.runs / hyperlinks / fieldSimples / alternateContents but not paragraph.contentControls. Text wrapped in inline SDT (common in docx output from external converters: pandoc / Quarto / LaTeX→docx wrap cross-ref placeholders [tab:foo] / [fig:bar] / [Smith 2020] in inline SDTs by convention) was silently 0-matched. Issue title 'literal [ ] brackets' was misleading — brackets are coincidence; bracket-free text inside inline SDTs failed too. Fix targets wrapper coverage gap, not bracket-as-regex-char escaping. ooxml-swift v0.20.4 adds TextReplacementEngine.replaceInContentXML (XML DOM walker that flattens <w:t> descendants of ContentControl.content with offset map and splices replacements back into <w:t> string content) + Document.replaceInContentControl (recursive helper for nested SDT children). Skipped during the walk: <w:delText> (TC deletion text), <w:instrText> (field instruction code), nested <w:sdt> subtrees (typed cc.children handled by outer recursion). Round-trip discipline: only <w:t> element string content is mutated; xml:space=preserve and other attributes survive verbatim. Differential test Issue63InlineSDTReplaceTests covers 4 wrappers (fldChar / fldSimple / hyperlink / inlineSDT) × 4 needles + nested SDT recursion + round-trip preservation. Suite: 690 → 693 ooxml-swift, 172 → 174 che-word-mcp. Bumps ooxml-swift v0.20.3 → v0.20.4. Backward compatible — surgical fix adds new code paths only. v3.14.3 sub-stack E of paragraph-level content-equality (closes #66): Paragraph.w14ParaId / w14TextId extract and round-trip the w14:* attributes on <w:p> opening tag — Word's revision-tracking GUIDs anchoring paragraph identity for collaborative editing and comment threading. Plain attribute passthrough with String? typing (Word's GUIDs are 8-char hex tokens, NOT RFC 4122 UUIDs). Pre-fix v3.14.2 silently dropped both attributes — accounted for ~95% of w14:* token loss in NTPU thesis fixture (2214 of 2359 lost tokens). Post-E measured impact: w14: retention 10.55% → 93.98%; document.xml size loss 10.95% → 8.02%. Combined with sub-stack D (#65), total impact since v3.14.1: <w:lang> 50% → 98.89% (D), w14:* 5% → 93.98% (E), document.xml size loss 16.66% → 8.02% (D+E, -8.64 pp). Matrix-pin testDocumentContentEqualityInvariant ratchets w14: floor 0.04 → 0.90; sizeLossRatio ceiling 0.12 → 0.10. The matrix-pin is now LOAD-BEARING across 5 preservation classes (rFonts / noProof / lang / kern / w14:) spanning run-level + paragraph-level + paragraph-mark scope. Defensive design (R2 review fixes): openingPTag() routes attributes through escapeXMLAttribute; parseParagraph rejects schema-invalid empty-string GUIDs. Bumps ooxml-swift v0.20.2 → v0.20.3. Backward compatible (both fields optional, default nil; openingPTag empty-attrs gate prevents synthetic emit). Suite: 686 → 690 pass / 0 fail / 1 skip. Architecture context: completes 'che-word-mcp-paragraph-level-content-equality' Spectra change (sub-stacks D + E). Remaining 8% loss is dominated by other w14:* attribute classes (e.g., w14:* on <w:r>) — tracked as separate follow-up SDD. v3.14.2 sub-stack D of paragraph-level content-equality (closes #65): ParagraphProperties.markRunProperties extracts and round-trips <w:rPr> direct child of <w:pPr> (paragraph-mark formatting per ECMA-376 §17.3.1.27 CT_PPrBase) — controls pilcrow ¶ glyph appearance. Reuses parseRunProperties verbatim — schema is identical to run-level CT_RPr, so all of sub-stack C's typed extraction (rFonts 4-axis, noProof, kern, lang 3-axis) and rawChildren passthrough (w14:* effects) come for free with zero schema duplication. Measured impact on NTPU thesis fixture: <w:lang> retention 50% → 98.89%, <w:rFonts> 88% → 98.77%, <w:noProof> 92% → 100%, <w:kern> 84% → 99.93%, document.xml size loss 16.66% → 10.95%. Matrix-pin testDocumentContentEqualityInvariant ratchets in lockstep (lang 0.45→0.95, rFonts/noProof/kern 0.95, sizeLossRatio ceiling 0.175→0.12). Sub-stack E (#66 w14:paraId/textId on <w:p>) ships next, completing the path to <5% round-trip loss. Bumps ooxml-swift v0.20.1→v0.20.2. Backward compatible (markRunProperties optional, default nil). v3.14.1 sub-stack C-CONT closes triple-confirmed P0 (R2 + R5 + Codex 6-AI verify): recognizedRprChildren Set listed ~16+ rPr child kinds as 'recognized' but parseRunProperties had NO extraction for them → silent drop on read. Affected: <w:spacing> (character spacing), <w:caps>/<w:smallCaps>, <w:position>, <w:shd> (run shading), <w:bdr>, <w:em> (CJK emphasis marks), <w:effect>, <w:vanish>/<w:specVanish>/<w:webHidden>, <w:outline>/<w:shadow>/<w:emboss>/<w:imprint>, <w:bCs>/<w:iCs>/<w:dstrike> — all very common in real Word docs. Fix: trim Set to ONLY actually-typed-extracted-or-emitted kinds. Everything else falls into rawChildren and round-trips byte-equivalent. Round-trip size loss: pre-fix v0.19.x 32% → v0.20.0 17.75% → v0.20.1 16.66%. Methodology lesson (6th): P2 from one reviewer can become P0 when another applies real-world impact lens. v3.14.0 closes #60 (sub-stack C of #58/#59/#60) — RunProperties field-loss audit. Adds typed fields: 4-axis rFonts (ascii/hAnsi/eastAsia/cs/hint — was collapsed to single value), noProof, kern, 3-axis lang (val/eastAsia/bidi), plus rawChildren passthrough for unrecognized rPr children (e.g., w14:textOutline / w14:textFill / w14:glow). Pre-fix MCP users saw eastAsia/cs fonts (e.g., DFKai-SB for traditional Chinese) silently replaced with ascii values on round-trip; v3.14.0 preserves all 4 axes independently. Matrix-pin testDocumentContentEqualityInvariant gains preservation-class-3 ratio-floor assertions making it LOAD-BEARING for any future RunProperties regression. Round-trip size loss for thesis fixture document.xml drops from pre-fix 32% to 17.75% (improvement of 14.25 percentage points); remaining 17.75% is paragraph-mark rPr + w14:paraId/textId drops (separate out-of-scope follow-up). Architectural completion of 'if not typed, preserve as raw' principle that started in sub-stack A (#58 BodyChild) and B (#59 WhitespaceOverlay). Backward compatible — legacy fontName field retained, mirrors rFonts.ascii. v3.13.13 CRITICAL HOTFIX (sub-stack B-CONT-2-CONT) reverted v3.13.12's TIER-0 over-fix that broke <w:del> round-trip. v3.13.12 (DO NOT USE — stripped <w:del> content). v3.13.11 sub-stack B-CONT (boundary check + 7 raw-capture sites + delText overlay + entity-decode + comments-trim removal). v3.13.10 sub-stack B initial WhitespaceOverlay implementation. v3.13.11: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.11 — sub-stack B-CONT (boundary check + 7 raw-capture sites + delText overlay + entity-decode + comments-trim removal). v3.13.10: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.10 — sub-stack B initial WhitespaceOverlay implementation (covers all 6 <w:t>-bearing parts). v3.13.10: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.10 — sub-stack B of #58/#59/#60 closes #59 initial WhitespaceOverlay implementation (covers all 6 <w:t>-bearing parts). v3.13.9: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.9 — A-CONT-3 mini-cycle of #58 closes 3 P0 from sub-stack A-CONT-2 6-AI verify INCLUDING SILENT CORRECTNESS REGRESSION (delete_bookmark for header/footer bookmarks was silently lost on disk in v3.13.8 — must-upgrade). Plus list_bookmarks now surfaces paragraph-level container bookmarks; insert_bookmark detects duplicate names across all 5 part types. Sub-cycle 4 for #58 (A → A-CONT → A-CONT-2 → A-CONT-3). Same convergence-cycle pattern as R5→R5-CONT-4 (5 sub-cycles for #56). v3.13.8: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.8 — A-CONT-2 mini-mini-cycle of #58 closes 2 P0 + 1 P1 from sub-stack A-CONT 6-AI verify (getBookmarks() now walks container bodyChildren across headers/footers/footnotes/endnotes; parseContainerChildBodyChildren now has 'case sdt' recursion mirroring parseBodyChildren; deleteBookmark() now symmetric with getBookmarks() — can delete names that getBookmarks() returns; matrix-pin synthetic fixture catches container parser regressions for real). Sub-cycle 3 for #58 — same convergence-cycle pattern as R5→R5-CONT-4 (5 sub-cycles for #56). v3.13.7: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.7 — A-CONT mini-cycle of #58 closes 2 P0 from sub-stack A 6-AI verify (parseContainerChildBodyChildren parser asymmetry: body-level bookmarks in headers/footers/footnotes/endnotes were still silently dropped post-v3.13.6; getBookmarks() walker missed body-level entries). Same convergence-cycle pattern as R5-CONT-4 (#56): per-task gate caught one parser entry point; 6-AI verify caught the symmetric sibling. v3.13.6: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.6 + word-to-md-swift to v0.6.1 — closes #58 (sub-stack A of #58/#59/#60 document-content-preservation): body-level <w:bookmarkStart>/<w:bookmarkEnd> (TOC _Toc<digits> anchors that wrap multiple paragraphs) now survive body-mutating save via BodyChild enum extension under 'if not typed, preserve as raw' principle. Cross-cutting matrix-pin testDocumentContentEqualityInvariant lands with bookmarkStart count parity (45=45 thesis fixture); B (#59 whitespace overlay) + C (#60 RunProperties audit) ship in subsequent v3.13.7/v3.14.0. v3.13.5: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.5 — closes R5 stack-completion (6 P0 + 5 P1 + R5-CONT 7 P0 + 5 P1 + R5-CONT-2 5 P0 + 4 P1 + R5-CONT-3 1 P0 + 4 P1 + R5-CONT-4 1 P0 + 3 P1 from #56 rounds 4-8 verify) via spectra-driven per-task verify gates (no che-word-mcp source changes). v0.19.4 was held back per verify-gate. Round 4 (R5 stack): walker symmetry across headers/footers/footnotes/endnotes — accept_revision / reject_revision / accept_all_revisions / reject_all_revisions / get_hyperlinks / replace_text now find wrappers in all parts. Round 5 (R5-CONT): per-container relationships round-trip — update_hyperlink URL sync targets owning part rels not document-scope. Round 6 (R5-CONT-2): delete_hyperlink mirror + reject_revision .insertion source-routing + container <w:tbl> capture preserved. Round 7 (R5-CONT-3): reject_revision typed-case clearMarker (file persistence converges with API state). Round 8 (R5-CONT-4): accept_revision typed-case clearMarker (mirror) + matrix-pin asymmetry-guard removal + Document.repairContainerFileNames marks document.xml.rels + [Content_Types].xml dirty after rename. Convergence: Devil's Advocate wrote 5 adversarial tests targeting the convergence-cycle pattern; all PASSED. v3.13.4: bumps ooxml-swift to v0.19.4 — held back per verify-gate, content rolled into v3.13.5. v3.13.3: bumped ooxml-swift to v0.19.3 (8 P0 + 3 P1 from #56 round 2 verify). v3.13.2: F1-F4 round 1 verify hot-fix. v3.13.1: pPr double-emission hot-fix. v3.13.0: document.xml lossless round-trip (closes #56, P0). v3.12.0: Programmatic Track Changes generation (#45). v3.11.0: Tables / Hyperlinks / Headers (#49 #50 #51). v3.10.0: Styles + Numbering + Sections (#46 #47 #48). v3.9.0: Content Controls (SDT) read/write (#44).
